markdown:
Good choice. The paper most directly centered on bidirectional Transformer encoders is **BERT**.

- **BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers for Language Understanding** (Devlin et al., 2018; arXiv v2 2019-05-24 / NAACL 2019, arXiv:1810.04805) — paper_id `bert_2018`.

Why this paper fits your goal, in its own words:

- It is defined explicitly as a bidirectional Transformer encoder: "BERT's model architecture is a multi-layer bidirectional Transformer encoder based on the original implementation described in Vaswani et al. (2017)." [1]
- Its core motivation is breaking the unidirectionality of prior models: "The major limitation is that standard language models are unidirectional… every token can only attend to previous tokens in the self-attention layers… Such restrictions are sub-optimal… and could be very harmful when applying fine-tuning based approaches to token-level tasks." [2]
- Bidirectionality is the central design property claimed: "BERT is designed to pretrain deep bidirectional representations from unlabeled text by jointly conditioning on both left and right context in all layers." [3]
- It operationalizes bidirectionality with the Masked Language Model (MLM) objective, which is what frees the encoder from a left-to-right constraint: "the MLM objective enables the representation to fuse the left and the right context, which allows us to pretrain a deep bidirectional Transformer." [4]
- The contrast with other pre-training architectures is laid out explicitly in Figure 3: "BERT uses a bidirectional Transformer.

Sources
[1] BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers for Language Understanding, 3 BERT, p. 3
[2] BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers for Language Understanding, 1 Introduction, p. 1
[3] BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers for Language Understanding, Abstract, p. 1
[4] BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers for Language Understanding, 1 Introduction, p. 1
